<div class="mb-2 tablet:mb-3 desktop:mb-4">

</div>

# Reduce cloud storage costs and improve operational efficiency with Datadog Storage Management

<div class="desktop:mb-9 tablet:mb-7 mb-5">

</div>

<div class="flex flex-wrap gap-2">

<a href="/blog/search/?tag=storage%20management" class="flex items-center gap-1 tablet:gap-1.5 desktop-sm:gap-2 h-fit group hover:text-white bg-grey-200 shadow-100 hover:bg-black active:bg-black rounded py-1 px-2 w-max button-roboto-mono" aria-label="storage management" data-value="storage management" title="storage management"><span class="block false">storage management</span></a><a href="/blog/search/?tag=aws" class="flex items-center gap-1 tablet:gap-1.5 desktop-sm:gap-2 h-fit group hover:text-white bg-grey-200 shadow-100 hover:bg-black active:bg-black rounded py-1 px-2 w-max button-roboto-mono" aria-label="aws" data-value="aws" title="aws"><span class="block false">aws</span></a><a href="/blog/search/?tag=amazon%20s3" class="flex items-center gap-1 tablet:gap-1.5 desktop-sm:gap-2 h-fit group hover:text-white bg-grey-200 shadow-100 hover:bg-black active:bg-black rounded py-1 px-2 w-max button-roboto-mono" aria-label="amazon s3" data-value="amazon s3" title="amazon s3"><span class="block false">amazon s3</span></a><a href="/blog/search/?tag=storage" class="flex items-center gap-1 tablet:gap-1.5 desktop-sm:gap-2 h-fit group hover:text-white bg-grey-200 shadow-100 hover:bg-black active:bg-black rounded py-1 px-2 w-max button-roboto-mono" aria-label="storage" data-value="storage" title="storage"><span class="block false">storage</span></a>

</div>

<div class="tablet:col-span-full tablet:grid tablet:grid-cols-12 tablet:gap-x-6 desktop-sm:grid-cols-9 desktop-sm:gap-x-10 markdown-content [&>*:not(.content-image-wrapper)]:tablet:col-span-12 [&>*:not(.content-image-wrapper)]:desktop-sm:col-span-7 [&>.interactive-diagram-component]:tablet:col-span-12 [&>.interactive-diagram-component]:desktop-sm:col-span-8 [&>form-wrapper]:tablet:col-span-12 [&>form-wrapper]:desktop-sm:col-span-8 [&>.expressive-code]:tablet:col-span-12 [&>.expressive-code]:desktop-sm:col-span-8 [&>.table-component]:tablet:col-span-12 [&>.table-component]:desktop-sm:col-span-8 [&>.dato-code-block-wrapper]:tablet:col-span-12 [&>.dato-code-block-wrapper]:desktop-sm:col-span-8 [&>.dast-content]:col-span-full">

<div class="dast-content tablet:grid tablet:grid-cols-subgrid [&>*:not(.content-image-wrapper)]:tablet:col-span-12 [&>*:not(.content-image-wrapper)]:desktop-sm:col-span-7 [&>.dato-code-block-wrapper]:desktop-sm:col-span-8 [&>.table-component]:desktop-sm:col-span-8 [&>.iframe-block-wrapper]:desktop-sm:col-span-8" astro-cid-twmmywjp="">

As organizations scale their cloud operations, object storage costs and performance issues often escalate. With petabytes of data, thousands of buckets, and millions of objects—especially from AI and data-intensive workloads—minor inefficiencies can quickly drive up expenses and degrade service quality.

[Datadog Storage Management](https://www.datadoghq.com/blog/datadog-storage-monitoring/) helps you see exactly how your cloud storage is used, reduce unnecessary costs, optimize performance, and improve operational efficiency across Amazon S3, Google Cloud Storage, and Azure Blob Storage. Storage Management now provides prefix-level metrics for Amazon S3 across three key use cases: cost optimization, performance optimization, and operational efficiency.

In this post, we’ll explore how new metrics for Amazon S3 in Storage Management help you:

- [Implement and manage Amazon S3 life cycle and retention policies](#implement-and-manage-s3-life-cycle-and-retention-policies)

- [Quickly identify opportunities to optimize Amazon S3 storage costs](#quickly-identify-opportunities-to-reduce-s3-storage-costs)

- [Identify high-traffic Amazon S3 buckets and resolve bottlenecks](#identify-high-traffic-s3-buckets-and-resolve-bottlenecks)

## [Implement and manage Amazon S3 life cycle and retention policies](#implement-and-manage-amazon-s3-life-cycle-and-retention-policies)

Configurations like versioning in Amazon S3 buckets provides valuable protection against accidental deletions and overwrites. But without proper life cycle management, version accumulation can lead to increased storage costs and management complexity. Storage Management provides visibility into bucket-level life cycle and retention policies directly within the Datadog platform, making it possible to understand existing configurations and identify opportunities for improvement—all in one place. This centralized view helps you enforce organization-wide storage policies without needing to switch between multiple AWS console pages.

For example, Storage Management might identify a versioned bucket accumulating excessive non-current versions without appropriate life cycle rules. The detailed metrics for this bucket would show the impact on storage consumption, along with current life cycle policy settings.

<div class="content-image-wrapper tablet:col-span-12 desktop-sm:col-span-8">

<div class="content-image-component">

<figure>
<img src="https://web-assets.dd-static.net/42588/1776304830-storage-management-storage-monitoring-1.png?format=auto&amp;fit=bounds&amp;quality=75&amp;disable=upscale&amp;width=1400&amp;dpr=1" title="Detailed view of an Amazon S3 bucket showing size, object count, requests, and latency over time for cassandra-backups." id="open-popup--1776304830-storage-management-storage-monitoring-1" class="h-auto max-w-full tablet:cursor-zoom-in" loading="lazy" width="2676" height="1244" alt="Detailed view of an Amazon S3 bucket showing size, object count, requests, and latency over time for cassandra-backups." />
</figure>

<span class="relative mx-auto [&>*:first-child]:bg-white block tablet:p-9 desktop-sm:p-12 rounded-3xl bg-white max-h-[calc(100vh-6rem)] overflow-y-auto" astro-cid-d4yttbaw=""> </span>

<figure class="content-image-component overflow-scroll max-h-[calc(100vh-12rem)]">
<img src="https://web-assets.dd-static.net/42588/1776304830-storage-management-storage-monitoring-1.png?format=auto&amp;fit=bounds&amp;quality=75&amp;disable=upscale&amp;width=1400&amp;dpr=1" class="w-full h-auto" loading="lazy" srcset="https://web-assets.dd-static.net/42588/1776304830-storage-management-storage-monitoring-1.png?format=auto&amp;fit=bounds&amp;quality=75&amp;disable=upscale&amp;width=1026&amp;dpr=1 1x, https://web-assets.dd-static.net/42588/1776304830-storage-management-storage-monitoring-1.png?format=auto&amp;fit=bounds&amp;quality=75&amp;disable=upscale&amp;width=1026&amp;dpr=1.5 1.5x, https://web-assets.dd-static.net/42588/1776304830-storage-management-storage-monitoring-1.png?format=auto&amp;fit=bounds&amp;quality=75&amp;disable=upscale&amp;width=1026&amp;dpr=2 2x, https://web-assets.dd-static.net/42588/1776304830-storage-management-storage-monitoring-1.png?format=auto&amp;fit=bounds&amp;quality=75&amp;disable=upscale&amp;width=1026&amp;dpr=2.5 2.5x, https://web-assets.dd-static.net/42588/1776304830-storage-management-storage-monitoring-1.png?format=auto&amp;fit=bounds&amp;quality=75&amp;disable=upscale&amp;width=1026&amp;dpr=3 3x, https://web-assets.dd-static.net/42588/1776304830-storage-management-storage-monitoring-1.png?format=auto&amp;fit=bounds&amp;quality=75&amp;disable=upscale&amp;width=1026&amp;dpr=3.5 3.5x, https://web-assets.dd-static.net/42588/1776304830-storage-management-storage-monitoring-1.png?format=auto&amp;fit=bounds&amp;quality=75&amp;disable=upscale&amp;width=1026&amp;dpr=4 4x" width="1026" alt="Detailed view of an Amazon S3 bucket showing size, object count, requests, and latency over time for cassandra-backups." />
</figure>

<div class="desktop-sm:p-6 absolute top-0 right-0 z-10 p-3" astro-cid-d4yttbaw="">

<span class="sr-only">Close dialog</span><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="h-6 w-6 fill-grey-400 hover:fill-purple-300 order-2 transition-default" />

</div>

</div>

</div>

With this visibility into your Amazon S3 bucket configurations, you can implement appropriate life cycle rules to manage non-current versions, moving them to lower-cost storage tiers or expiring them after appropriate retention periods.

## [Quickly identify opportunities to reduce Amazon S3 storage costs](#quickly-identify-opportunities-to-reduce-amazon-s3-storage-costs)

Organizations often store data in the Amazon S3 Standard storage class by default, but this tier isn’t cost-effective for infrequently accessed data. Identifying which data can be moved to lower-cost storage tiers typically requires deep analysis of access patterns and object metadata—especially on a prefix level.

Storage Management’s new request metrics by prefix for Amazon S3 enable you to visualize exactly the prefixes that are actively used and, conversely, those that contain rarely accessed data. Combined with object count and age metrics, you can quickly identify opportunities to transition infrequently accessed data to lower-cost storage classes.

Let’s say you have a workload that’s fine-tuning an LLM model. Storage Management can show that your training dataset prefixes contain terabytes of data that were heavily accessed during initial training but haven’t been retrieved since the model was deployed five months ago. In the following example screenshot, a specific AI dataset prefix is selected, and the sidepanel shows that the objects in that prefix haven’t been accessed in over a month.

<div class="content-image-wrapper tablet:col-span-12 desktop-sm:col-span-8">

<div class="content-image-component">

<figure>
<img src="https://web-assets.dd-static.net/42588/1776304834-storage-management-storage-monitoring-2z.png?format=auto&amp;fit=bounds&amp;quality=75&amp;disable=upscale&amp;width=1400&amp;dpr=1" title="Amazon S3 prefix details showing storage size, object count, cost, and timeseries request activity over several months." id="open-popup--1776304834-storage-management-storage-monitoring-2z" class="h-auto max-w-full tablet:cursor-zoom-in" loading="lazy" width="1800" height="426" alt="Amazon S3 prefix details showing storage size, object count, cost, and timeseries request activity over several months." />
</figure>

<span class="relative mx-auto [&>*:first-child]:bg-white block tablet:p-9 desktop-sm:p-12 rounded-3xl bg-white max-h-[calc(100vh-6rem)] overflow-y-auto" astro-cid-d4yttbaw=""> </span>

<figure class="content-image-component overflow-scroll max-h-[calc(100vh-12rem)]">
<img src="https://web-assets.dd-static.net/42588/1776304834-storage-management-storage-monitoring-2z.png?format=auto&amp;fit=bounds&amp;quality=75&amp;disable=upscale&amp;width=1400&amp;dpr=1" class="w-full h-auto" loading="lazy" srcset="https://web-assets.dd-static.net/42588/1776304834-storage-management-storage-monitoring-2z.png?format=auto&amp;fit=bounds&amp;quality=75&amp;disable=upscale&amp;width=1026&amp;dpr=1 1x, https://web-assets.dd-static.net/42588/1776304834-storage-management-storage-monitoring-2z.png?format=auto&amp;fit=bounds&amp;quality=75&amp;disable=upscale&amp;width=1026&amp;dpr=1.5 1.5x, https://web-assets.dd-static.net/42588/1776304834-storage-management-storage-monitoring-2z.png?format=auto&amp;fit=bounds&amp;quality=75&amp;disable=upscale&amp;width=1026&amp;dpr=2 2x, https://web-assets.dd-static.net/42588/1776304834-storage-management-storage-monitoring-2z.png?format=auto&amp;fit=bounds&amp;quality=75&amp;disable=upscale&amp;width=1026&amp;dpr=2.5 2.5x, https://web-assets.dd-static.net/42588/1776304834-storage-management-storage-monitoring-2z.png?format=auto&amp;fit=bounds&amp;quality=75&amp;disable=upscale&amp;width=1026&amp;dpr=3 3x, https://web-assets.dd-static.net/42588/1776304834-storage-management-storage-monitoring-2z.png?format=auto&amp;fit=bounds&amp;quality=75&amp;disable=upscale&amp;width=1026&amp;dpr=3.5 3.5x, https://web-assets.dd-static.net/42588/1776304834-storage-management-storage-monitoring-2z.png?format=auto&amp;fit=bounds&amp;quality=75&amp;disable=upscale&amp;width=1026&amp;dpr=4 4x" width="1026" alt="Amazon S3 prefix details showing storage size, object count, cost, and timeseries request activity over several months." />
</figure>

<div class="desktop-sm:p-6 absolute top-0 right-0 z-10 p-3" astro-cid-d4yttbaw="">

<span class="sr-only">Close dialog</span><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="h-6 w-6 fill-grey-400 hover:fill-purple-300 order-2 transition-default" />

</div>

</div>

</div>

These metrics enable you to identify exactly which data would benefit from transitioning to Glacier or Glacier Instant Retrieval and, as a result, implement appropriate life cycle policies through the Amazon S3 console or AWS CLI.

## [Identify high-traffic Amazon S3 buckets and resolve bottlenecks](#identify-high-traffic-amazon-s3-buckets-and-resolve-bottlenecks)

Performance issues such as throttling or latency are often caused by hot prefixes, which are paths that receive more requests than Amazon S3 can efficiently handle. But pinpointing these hotspots requires granular visibility that most teams don’t have. Storage Management’s new latency by prefix metrics provide unprecedented visibility into exactly where performance bottlenecks are occurring.

For example, Storage Management might show that a specific prefix in your media processing bucket is experiencing high latency during peak processing times. Using the Summary graph, you can identify high-traffic buckets and then zero in on the corresponding prefix. In the screenshot below, the treemap breaks down latency by prefix within the `storage-monitoring-test-saleel` bucket. The largest blocks represent prefixes with the highest average latency, such as `tb` and `tb/firstlevel`.

<div class="content-image-wrapper tablet:col-span-12 desktop-sm:col-span-8">

<div class="content-image-component">

<figure>
<img src="https://web-assets.dd-static.net/42588/1776304838-storage-management-storage-monitoring-3x.png?format=auto&amp;fit=bounds&amp;quality=75&amp;disable=upscale&amp;width=1400&amp;dpr=1" title="Treemap view of Amazon S3 bucket latency, highlighting large contributors like observability-graph and eventstore-logs." id="open-popup--1776304838-storage-management-storage-monitoring-3x" class="h-auto max-w-full tablet:cursor-zoom-in" loading="lazy" width="1800" height="354" alt="Treemap view of Amazon S3 bucket latency, highlighting large contributors like observability-graph and eventstore-logs." />
</figure>

<span class="relative mx-auto [&>*:first-child]:bg-white block tablet:p-9 desktop-sm:p-12 rounded-3xl bg-white max-h-[calc(100vh-6rem)] overflow-y-auto" astro-cid-d4yttbaw=""> </span>

<figure class="content-image-component overflow-scroll max-h-[calc(100vh-12rem)]">
<img src="https://web-assets.dd-static.net/42588/1776304838-storage-management-storage-monitoring-3x.png?format=auto&amp;fit=bounds&amp;quality=75&amp;disable=upscale&amp;width=1400&amp;dpr=1" class="w-full h-auto" loading="lazy" srcset="https://web-assets.dd-static.net/42588/1776304838-storage-management-storage-monitoring-3x.png?format=auto&amp;fit=bounds&amp;quality=75&amp;disable=upscale&amp;width=1026&amp;dpr=1 1x, https://web-assets.dd-static.net/42588/1776304838-storage-management-storage-monitoring-3x.png?format=auto&amp;fit=bounds&amp;quality=75&amp;disable=upscale&amp;width=1026&amp;dpr=1.5 1.5x, https://web-assets.dd-static.net/42588/1776304838-storage-management-storage-monitoring-3x.png?format=auto&amp;fit=bounds&amp;quality=75&amp;disable=upscale&amp;width=1026&amp;dpr=2 2x, https://web-assets.dd-static.net/42588/1776304838-storage-management-storage-monitoring-3x.png?format=auto&amp;fit=bounds&amp;quality=75&amp;disable=upscale&amp;width=1026&amp;dpr=2.5 2.5x, https://web-assets.dd-static.net/42588/1776304838-storage-management-storage-monitoring-3x.png?format=auto&amp;fit=bounds&amp;quality=75&amp;disable=upscale&amp;width=1026&amp;dpr=3 3x, https://web-assets.dd-static.net/42588/1776304838-storage-management-storage-monitoring-3x.png?format=auto&amp;fit=bounds&amp;quality=75&amp;disable=upscale&amp;width=1026&amp;dpr=3.5 3.5x, https://web-assets.dd-static.net/42588/1776304838-storage-management-storage-monitoring-3x.png?format=auto&amp;fit=bounds&amp;quality=75&amp;disable=upscale&amp;width=1026&amp;dpr=4 4x" width="1026" alt="Treemap view of Amazon S3 bucket latency, highlighting large contributors like observability-graph and eventstore-logs." />
</figure>

<div class="desktop-sm:p-6 absolute top-0 right-0 z-10 p-3" astro-cid-d4yttbaw="">

<span class="sr-only">Close dialog</span><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="h-6 w-6 fill-grey-400 hover:fill-purple-300 order-2 transition-default" />

</div>

</div>

</div>

These detailed metrics can provide clues to help you implement design patterns that will better distribute the workload, such as using randomized prefixes to avoid throttling.

## [Get granular visibility into storage usage and patterns](#get-granular-visibility-into-storage-usage-and-patterns)

Storage Management helps you understand exactly how your Amazon S3 storage is being used with granular metrics that enable you to optimize costs, performance, and operational efficiency. Each metric provides clear visibility into your storage usage patterns and configurations, enabling you to make informed decisions.

Storage Management for Amazon S3 is available today, along with object size metrics for Google Cloud Storage and Azure Blob Storage. Request-related metrics for Google Cloud Storage and Azure Blob Storage are coming soon. To learn more, see the [Storage Management documentation](https://docs.datadoghq.com/integrations/guide/storage-monitoring-setup/). And if you’re not yet a Datadog user, you can <a href="#" class="signup-trigger">get started with a 14-day free trial</a>.

</div>

</div>

<div class="desktop-sm:col-span-full desktop-sm:mb-12 mb-9">

</div>

<div class="desktop-sm:col-span-3 desktop-sm:col-start-1 desktop-sm:row-start-1 hidden desktop-sm:flex flex-col justify-between desktop-sm:pr-10">

Reduce cloud storage costs and improve operational efficiency with Datadog Storage Management

- <a href="#implement-and-manage-amazon-s3-life-cycle-and-retention-policies" class="inline-block font-national font-normal border-l-2 hover:text-purple-700 border-grey-300 desktop-sm:pl-3 hover:border-purple-300 [.active&gt;&amp;]:border-purple-600 active:border-purple-600 [.active&gt;&amp;]:text-purple-600 group-has-[.active]:text-purple-600 group-has-[.active]:border-purple-600 active:text-purple-800 [.active&gt;&amp;]:font-bold group-has-[.active]:font-bold group-has-[.active]:hover:border-purple-700 group-has-[.active]:hover:text-purple-700 [.active]:hover:border-purple-700 [.active&gt;&amp;]:hover:text-purple-700 [.active]:active:border-purple-800 [.active&gt;&amp;]:active:text-purple-800" data-dd-action-name="toc: heading: Implement and manage Amazon S3 life cycle and retention policies">Implement and manage Amazon S3 life cycle and retention policies</a>
- <a href="#quickly-identify-opportunities-to-reduce-amazon-s3-storage-costs" class="inline-block font-national font-normal border-l-2 hover:text-purple-700 border-grey-300 desktop-sm:pl-3 hover:border-purple-300 [.active&gt;&amp;]:border-purple-600 active:border-purple-600 [.active&gt;&amp;]:text-purple-600 group-has-[.active]:text-purple-600 group-has-[.active]:border-purple-600 active:text-purple-800 [.active&gt;&amp;]:font-bold group-has-[.active]:font-bold group-has-[.active]:hover:border-purple-700 group-has-[.active]:hover:text-purple-700 [.active]:hover:border-purple-700 [.active&gt;&amp;]:hover:text-purple-700 [.active]:active:border-purple-800 [.active&gt;&amp;]:active:text-purple-800" data-dd-action-name="toc: heading: Quickly identify opportunities to reduce Amazon S3 storage costs">Quickly identify opportunities to reduce Amazon S3 storage costs</a>
